detail: Connected the deterministic VRL trace to a stub prover pipeline without introducing network calls or hidden state.
Mapped each deterministic landed-cost trace step into a named circuit constraint family.
Confirmed that repeated executions produce the same input hash, output hash, trace hash, and proof hex.
Documented the shared repository topology for coordinator, agents, zk, backend, infra, tests, and memory.
Benchmarked the stub proving path to establish a baseline before a real proving backend is introduced.
Recommended a plonkish proving path first, with STARK evaluation kept as a later upgrade path for transparency and scale.
Recorded the stub proof boundary explicitly so later agents do not mistake deterministic hashing for a deployed ZK proof system.
Executed deterministic same-input and varied-input pipeline simulations to validate the first cycle.
Validated a deterministic verifier path that can later be replaced by a real on-chain or off-chain verifier backend.
Selected a layered architecture that keeps deterministic VRL computation separate from future prover backends.
Connected the deterministic VRL trace to a stub prover pipeline without introducing network calls or hidden state.

Selected a layered architecture that keeps deterministic VRL computation separate from future prover backends.
Connected the deterministic VRL trace to typed trace and witness artifacts without introducing network calls or hidden state.
Mapped each deterministic landed-cost trace step into a typed artifact-compatible circuit constraint family.
Confirmed that repeated executions produce the same trace, witness, and final proof artifacts.
Documented the shared repository topology and verified it against the current proof pipeline state.
Produced a structured deterministic proof envelope with commitments, public inputs, and prover metadata.
Recommended a plonkish proving path first, while preserving compatibility with future verifier upgrades.
Injected malformed traces, corrupted witnesses, and replayed proofs to ensure invalid artifact states are rejected.
Executed deterministic same-input and varied-input pipeline simulations against the typed artifact flow.
Validated a structured verifier path that can later be replaced by a real on-chain or off-chain verifier backend.
Selected a layered architecture that keeps deterministic VRL computation separate from future prover backends.
